source_paper_title: Risk Sharing with Lambda Value at Risk
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Liu_2025, title={Risk Sharing with Lambda Value at Risk}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0246}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0246}, number={1}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Liu, Peng}, year={2025}, month=feb, pages={313–333} }
context:
Section 1 Introduction (page 3) and Section 6 Conclusion (page 17).

The inf-convolution of two \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) is a very difficult problem and is beyond the scope of this paper as it involves the robust risk aggregation of two \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) with fixed marginal distributions and unknown dependence structure.

There are still some unsolved problems such as the inf-convolution of multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\), and the inf-convolution of multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) under heterogenous beliefs.
title: Characterize inf-convolution of multiple Lambda value-at-risk plus risk measures setting
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be an atomless probability space and let \(\mathcal X\) be a linear space of real-valued random variables (losses) with \(L^\infty\subseteq \mathcal X\subseteq L^1\). For \(X\in\mathcal X\), write \(F_X\) for its distribution function and \(F_X^{-1}(p)=\inf\{x\in\mathbb R: F_X(x)\ge p\}\) for its left-quantile, \(p\in(0,1]\). An \(n\)-allocation of an aggregate loss \(X\) is an \(n\)-tuple \((X_1,\dots,X_n)\in\mathcal X^n\) with \(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i=X\) almost surely.

A (not necessarily cash-additive) risk measure is a map \(\rho:\mathcal X\to(-\infty,\infty]\). Given risk measures \(\rho_1,\dots,\rho_n\), their inf-convolution is
\[\big(\square_{i=1}^n \rho_i\big)(X)=\inf\Big\{\sum_{i=1}^n \rho_i(X_i): (X_1,\dots,X_n)\in\mathcal X^n,\ \sum_{i=1}^n X_i=X\Big\}.\]

Fix a right-continuous function \(\Lambda:\mathbb R\to[0,1]\) that is not identically equal to \(1\). Define the Lambda value-at-risk plus functional by
\[\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+(X)=\sup\{x\in\mathbb R: F_X(x)< 1-\Lambda(x)\},\]
with the conventions \(\sup\emptyset=-\infty\) and \(\inf\emptyset=\infty\) (when needed). For multiple agents, allow \(\Lambda_i\) and corresponding \(\Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\), \(i=1,\dots,n\).

The paper studies risk sharing via inf-convolution for \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) and for \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) combined with another risk measure under additional structure, but explicitly states that the case of inf-convolving multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) remains open.
problem_definition_latex:
Given \(n\ge 2\) right-continuous functions \(\Lambda_i:\mathbb R\to[0,1]\) (not identically \(1\)) and the associated risk measures \(\rho_i=\Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\) on \(\mathcal X\), determine (i) an explicit or tractable characterization of the inf-convolution
\[\big(\square_{i=1}^n \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\big)(X)=\inf\Big\{\sum_{i=1}^n \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+(X_i): (X_1,\dots,X_n)\in\mathcal X^n,\ \sum_{i=1}^n X_i=X\Big\}\]
and (ii) the structure (and existence conditions) of optimal allocations \((X_1,\dots,X_n)\) attaining the infimum, as functions of \(X\) and \((\Lambda_1,\dots,\Lambda_n)\).
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be an atomless probability space and let \(\mathcal X\) be a linear space of real-valued random variables (losses) with \(L^\infty\subseteq \mathcal X\subseteq L^1\). For \(X\in\mathcal X\), write \(F_X\) for its distribution function and \(F_X^{-1}(p)=\inf\{x\in\mathbb R: F_X(x)\ge p\}\) for its left-quantile, \(p\in(0,1]\). An \(n\)-allocation of an aggregate loss \(X\) is an \(n\)-tuple \((X_1,\dots,X_n)\in\mathcal X^n\) with \(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i=X\) almost surely.

A (not necessarily cash-additive) risk measure is a map \(\rho:\mathcal X\to(-\infty,\infty]\). Given risk measures \(\rho_1,\dots,\rho_n\), their inf-convolution is
\[\big(\square_{i=1}^n \rho_i\big)(X)=\inf\Big\{\sum_{i=1}^n \rho_i(X_i): (X_1,\dots,X_n)\in\mathcal X^n,\ \sum_{i=1}^n X_i=X\Big\}.\]

Fix a right-continuous function \(\Lambda:\mathbb R\to[0,1]\) that is not identically equal to \(1\). Define the Lambda value-at-risk plus functional by
\[\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+(X)=\sup\{x\in\mathbb R: F_X(x)< 1-\Lambda(x)\},\]

Given \(n\ge 2\) right-continuous functions \(\Lambda_i:\mathbb R\to[0,1]\) (not identically \(1\)) and the associated risk measures \(\rho_i=\Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\) on \(\mathcal X\), determine (i) an explicit or tractable characterization of the inf-convolution
\[\big(\square_{i=1}^n \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\big)(X)=\inf\Big\{\sum_{i=1}^n \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+(X_i): (X_1,\dots,X_n)\in\mathcal X^n,\ \sum_{i=1}^n X_i=X\Big\}\]
and (ii) the structure (and existence conditions) of optimal allocations \((X_1,\dots,X_n)\) attaining the infimum, as functions of \(X\) and \((\Lambda_1,\dots,\Lambda_n)\).
open_problem_quote:
The inf-convolution of two \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) is a very difficult problem and is beyond the scope of this paper as it involves the robust risk aggregation of two \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) with fixed marginal distributions and unknown dependence structure.

There are still some unsolved problems such as the inf-convolution of multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\), and the inf-convolution of multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) under heterogenous beliefs.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can numerically search over finite-dimensional parametric families of \(\Lambda_i\), discrete loss distributions, and dependence couplings to probe candidate formulas or conjectured allocation structures, but the universal quantification over all \(X\) and couplings makes definitive counterexamples less straightforward.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate constructions (e.g., allocation rules, bounds, or conjectured closed forms for restricted classes) can be represented programmatically and evaluated by computing \(\sum_i \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+(X_i)\) under constraints \(\sum_i X_i=X\) on discretized spaces; however, the inner optimization and dependence-robust aspects can make the fitness signal expensive and only approximate the full general problem.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Liu, \"Risk sharing with Lambda value at risk\", 2023) solves the inf-convolution problem for multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) (Definition (4)) in several regimes (all \(\Lambda_i\) increasing, all decreasing, or general right-continuous \(\Lambda_i\) under additional conditions). In these cases the inf-convolution reduces to an optimization over deterministic shifts \(x_1,\dots,x_{n-1}\) of a composite \(\Lambda^{x_{n-1}}\) (equations (7)/(9)/(14)), and under attainability/continuity assumptions it collapses further to a single \(\Lambda^*\) via \(\Lambda^*(x)=\sup_{\sum x_i=x}\big(\sum_i \Lambda_i(x_i)\big)\wedge 1\), yielding \(\square_i \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}=\Lambda^*\mathrm{VaR}\). Optimal allocations are explicit and have the familiar ‘probability-space partition’ structure known from VaR/RVaR risk sharing (Embrechts--Liu--Wang 2018).

For \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) (Definition (5)), Liu (2023) obtains a tractable characterization only when inf-convolving \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) with an SSD-consistent risk measure \(\rho\) (Theorem 5). The resulting formula already involves a robust aggregation component: one optimizes over parameters \((x,y)\) and evaluates \(\rho\big(X-F^{-1}_{x,y}(U_X)\big)\), where \(F_{x,y}\) splices the constraint \(1-\Lambda\) into a distributional construction. The paper explicitly states that the inf-convolution of two (or more) \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) is beyond scope because it requires solving a dependence-uncertainty/robust risk aggregation problem with fixed marginals.

Accordingly, the open problem remains unsolved in general: there is currently no explicit analogue of \(\Lambda^*\) or a general optimal-allocation structure for \(\square_{i=1}^n \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\) when the \(\Lambda_i\) are not all increasing (the increasing case collapses to \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\)). Promising directions suggested by the existing literature are to (a) derive sharp upper/lower bounds for \(\square_i \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\) using quantile aggregation/convolution bounds under dependence uncertainty (e.g., Blanchet--Lam--Liu--Wang), and (b) identify special classes of \(\Lambda_i\) (e.g., step functions, piecewise-constant tail constraints) where the robust aggregation problem becomes finite-dimensional and may admit explicit extremal couplings, paralleling known results for VaR aggregation (Wang--Wang; Jakobsons--Han--Wang).

sources.title: Risk sharing with Lambda value at risk
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops explicit inf-convolution formulas and optimal allocations for multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) under monotone \(\Lambda\), and for \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) inf-convolved with an SSD-consistent risk measure. Explicitly identifies the inf-convolution of multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) as open and relates it to robust risk aggregation under dependence uncertainty.
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem: characterizing \(\square_{i=1}^n \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\) and optimal allocations. It provides the closest available partial results (Theorem 5) for \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\square \rho\) and explains the main obstacle: robust aggregation with fixed marginals and unknown dependence.

sources.title: Risk Measures on P(R) and Value at Risk with Probability/Loss function
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) and \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) (via probability/loss functions), studies their basic properties (monotonicity, quasi-convexity, cash-(sub)additivity issues), and motivates \(\Lambda\)-dependent tail control beyond classical VaR.
sources.connection: Provides the foundational definitions and properties of \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) used in the open problem; clarifies when \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}=\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) (e.g., increasing \(\Lambda\)) and why \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) is tail-sensitive for decreasing \(\Lambda\).

sources.title: An axiomatization of Λ-quantiles
sources.theorem_or_result: Axiomatizes \(\Lambda\)-quantiles and compares alternative definitions; proves equivalences and separation results (e.g., when \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}=\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\)), and studies properties for increasing \(\Lambda\).
sources.connection: Helps delineate regimes where the open problem collapses to the already-solved \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) inf-convolution (e.g., increasing \(\Lambda_i\)), and highlights subtleties of \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) for non-increasing \(\Lambda\).

sources.title: Quantile-based risk sharing
sources.theorem_or_result: Derives analytical inf-convolution formulas and optimal allocations for quantile-based risk measures including VaR and RVaR; shows optimal allocations can be chosen via probability-space partitions and provides explicit expressions.
sources.connection: Serves as the template for inf-convolution techniques used by Liu (2023) for \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\). For \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\), it provides the closest analogous results for VaR-type functionals and highlights where dependence-uncertainty/robust aggregation enters.

sources.title: Inf-convolution, optimal allocations, and model uncertainty for tail risk measures
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies inf-convolution and optimal allocations for tail risk measures (including VaR-type) under model uncertainty; develops techniques using tail-distribution invariance and robust aggregation bounds.
sources.connection: Provides methodological tools (tail risk measures, dependence uncertainty) that Liu (2023) leverages in Theorem 5 for \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\square \rho\). These tools are likely needed to tackle multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) where robust aggregation is central.

sources.title: Cash-subadditive risk measures without quasi-convexity
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops representations and structural results for cash-subadditive (non-cash-additive) risk measures, including \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\)-type functionals, and links them to VaR-based representations.
sources.connection: Supports the \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}\) side of the theory and provides representation tools (used in Liu (2023), Proposition 3) that may help reduce \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) problems to quantile aggregation/robust bounds in special cases.

sources.title: Convolution bounds on quantile aggregation
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides sharp bounds and methods for quantile aggregation under dependence uncertainty (convolution/Fréchet-type bounds), relevant to robust VaR aggregation problems.
sources.connection: Liu (2023) notes that multiple \(\Lambda\mathrm{VaR}^+\) inf-convolution entails robust aggregation with fixed marginals and unknown dependence. Quantile aggregation bounds are a key ingredient for any tractable characterization or bounds for \(\square_i \Lambda_i\mathrm{VaR}^+\).
